---
# Explode a JSON-array column and count occurrences of each element

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

When a column holds a JSON array string (`roles_json = '["admin","vpn"]'`), use `json_extract_scalar_array(col, "$")` — the `"$"` path is for a column that IS itself a top-level array (use `"$.key"` only for an array nested under a key). It returns UNQUOTED scalars so a later `comp by` matches correctly (prefer it over `json_extract_array`, whose elements are quote-wrapped and silently mismatch). `arrayexpand` then emits one row per element to count.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| alter roles = json_extract_scalar_array(roles_json, "$")
| arrayexpand roles
| comp count() as users_with_role by roles
| sort desc users_with_role
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
